paneContent:
space after fixed and auto-sized columns are accounted for, while `max-content` means 
  the column takes up exactly what its content needs.

  The simplest solution might be to just prevent `.cellContent` from shrinking and let 
  the grid handle the sizing naturally. Or I could measure after render and set minimum 
  sizes via CSS custom properties.

  What the user actually needs is: in horizontal layout, each cell should be as wide as 
  its content requires, but all cells should match the width of the widest one; in 
  vertical layout, the same applies to height. CSS Grid with `minmax(max-content, 1fr)` 
  should handle this perfectly.

  Actually, I realize CSS Grid doesn't have a built-in way to enforce "all columns must 
  be at least as wide as the widest column's content" — `1fr` divides available space 
  equally, but columns can still shrink below their content size if the container is too
  small.

  The JavaScript approach using `useLayoutEffect` and `ResizeObserver` is more reliable:
  I'll measure each `.cellContent` element's natural dimensions after render, track the 
  maximum width and height, then apply those as CSS custom properties to enforce 
  consistent sizing across all cells.

  Implementing this now...

◐ The issue is clear. Currently cells use `flex: 1 1 0` which distributes space   regardless of content size. The user wants cells to snap to the largest compon  natural size — so all cells are at least as wide/tall as the biggest component
  The approach:
  1. After all components render, measure each cell's content natural size
  2. Set the grid to use the max width/height as the cell size
  3. For horizontal layout: all cells get the same width (max of all content wid  heights stretch to fill


  4. For vertical layout: all cells get the same height (max of all content heig  widths stretch to fill

  I'll use a ResizeObserver to measure all the cell content elements after they   then calculate the maximum dimensions across all cells and apply those as unif  sizing constraints. all

◉ Fixing ComponentSet cell sizing (Esc to cancel · 1.1 KiB)
  └ 155 lines read

● Read ComponentSetPage.module.css
  │ packages/react/src/story/ComponentSetPage.module.css
  └ 114 lines read

◐ The issue is clear. Currently cells use `flex: 1 1 0` which distributes space equally 
  regardless of content size. The user wants cells to snap to the largest component's 
  natural size — so all cells are at least as wide/tall as the biggest component.

  The approach:
  1. After all components render, measure each cell's content natural size
  2. Set the grid to use the max width/height as the cell size
  3. For horizontal layout: all cells get the same width (max of all content widths), 
  heights stretch to fill


  4. For vertical layout: all cells get the same height (max of all content heights), 
  widths stretch to fill

  I'll use a ResizeObserver to measure all the cell content elements after they render, 
  then calculate the maximum dimensions across all cells and apply those as uniform 
  sizing constraints. all

  - `grid-template-columns: repeat(N, 1fr)` makes all columns equal width — but based on   the container, not content
  - `grid-template-columns: repeat(N, max-content)` sizes each column to its content 
  independently — not what we want
  - `grid-template-columns: repeat(N, minmax(max-content, 1fr))` — this would make all 
  columns at least as wide as the widest content

  Wait, I need to reconsider how `1fr` works. It represents an equal share of remaining 
  space after fixed and auto-sized columns are accounted for, while `max-content` means 
  the column takes up exactly what its content needs.

  should handle this perfectly.
